findings.description: Digital signature metadata rendered as visible text in the HTML, inserting content not visually present in the PDF between the 'From' block and the 'Subject' block.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows 'Solid Waste Section' followed directly by 'Subject: Meeting Officer's Report'. HTML inserts three additional lines between them: 'Digitally signed by LYF', 'DN: cn=LYF, o=DWM, ou=SWS, email=larry.frost@ncdenr.gov, c=US', and 'Date: 2016.05.12 12:11:52 -04:00'. These are not visible in the PDF rendering.

findings.description: The handwritten signature image next to 'From: Larry Frost' is present in the PDF but has no corresponding tag in the HTML. Signatures are substantive images per the audit criteria.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows a handwritten signature graphic adjacent to the 'From:' line. The HTML only contains the digital-signature metadata text ('Digitally signed by LYF…') but no element for the actual signature graphic.

findings.description: PDF page-footer content (agency address and phone number) is inserted into the HTML body flow between the table of contents and Section I, breaking reading flow.
findings.evidence: HTML contains a <footer> block with 'State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Waste Management … 919 707 8200 T' placed between the <nav> (TOC) and the <h2 id="history-background"> heading. This is page-footer boilerplate from the PDF dropped mid-content rather than consolidated outside the body flow.
